query: I'm building my eww bar. Left side I want my workspaces and the window title, center is the clock, and on the right I need network, battery, volume and the systray. I tried centerbox but it's throwing an error. Here's roughly what I have: (centerbox :orientation "h" (workspaces) (title) (clock) (network) (battery) (volume) (systray)). What's wrong?
expected_behavior: Identifies that centerbox requires EXACTLY 3 children, not more or fewer

Fixes it by wrapping the left group (workspaces + title) in a box and the right group (network/battery/volume/systray) in another box, leaving clock as the single center child — so the centerbox gets exactly 3 children
notes: Guards against the naive answer that treats centerbox like a flexible box accepting any number of children. The skill documents the hard 3-children constraint and the standard fix (group zones in a box). A wrong answer would suggest hexpand spacers inside a single box, which the skill explicitly calls out as fragile (center drifts).
